seoTitle: How to Fully Remove a Google Workspace Account 2026
metaDescription: Shutting down Google Workspace? Here's how to delete your organization, remove users, cancel billing, and release your domain safely in 2026.
intro: Closing out a Google Workspace account involves more than just canceling a subscription — you need to remove users, stop billing, and decide what happens to your domain and data. This guide walks through the process using the Google Admin Console so freelancers, startups, and small businesses can shut down a Workspace organization cleanly. It also covers backing up your data before anything gets deleted.

steps.heading: Back up your data before deleting anything
steps.body: Download any emails, files, or Drive content you need to keep, since removing the Workspace account can permanently delete data tied to it.

steps.heading: Remove users from the organization
steps.body: Go into the Google Admin Console and remove or suspend user accounts as part of winding down the Workspace organization.

steps.heading: Cancel your Google Workspace billing
steps.body: Cancel the subscription in the billing section of the Admin Console to stop future charges before completing the account removal.

steps.heading: Delete the Workspace organization
steps.body: Use the Admin Console's account deletion option to remove the Workspace organization itself once users and billing are handled.

steps.heading: Release your domain if needed
steps.body: If you want to reuse the domain elsewhere or with a different provider, release it from Google Workspace as part of the shutdown process.

faq.question: Will I lose my data if I delete my Google Workspace account?
faq.answer: Yes, deleting the account can permanently remove associated data, which is why the guide recommends downloading anything important before starting the removal process.

faq.question: Do I need to cancel billing separately from deleting the account?
faq.answer: Yes — canceling billing and deleting the Workspace organization are treated as separate steps, and skipping billing cancellation risks being charged again.

faq.question: Can I keep using my domain after removing Google Workspace?
faq.answer: Yes, releasing your domain from Workspace lets you continue using it with another email provider or purpose once the account is shut down.

faq.question: Is this process different for a free trial versus a paid account?
faq.answer: The tutorial covers shutting down accounts whether they started as a trial, a small project, or full business use, though canceling a trial may involve fewer billing steps.
